threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the Overground' when waiting for the Dalston Junction train. Proper locals know it's all in the branding.
Heard a guy on the 333 last night trying to serenade the driver with his own version of 'Wonderwall'—he's lucky it didn't turn into a duet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: As I walked past the tempelhof park, I smelled freshly roasted coffee mixed with the unmistakable scent of street food; that’s when I knew I belonged.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see a spontaneous concert break out in a U-Bahn station during a strike, with people dancing on the platforms. It’s pure magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they’ll walk past a barking dog on the train tracks but stand frozen at the coffee shop for 15 minutes deciding on a pour-over.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 9 at Civic Center, stopped for a dog on the tracks. We sat in awkward silence, everyone smiling at the pup instead of each other.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all sparrow songs are the same, but if you sit in Vallcarca, you'll hear them mimicking the church bells—a true local serenade.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled look at the bus schedule. Locals know it's a guideline, not a timetable.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus is a backup plan for the Tube. Real locals know it's the main event, especially with those strikes coming up.
Last Friday, caught a night bus home and a guy was passionately debating whether a kebab counts as a 'proper meal' at 3 AM. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting on the tram, a group of kids started a spontaneous rap battle. Only in Neukölln would the whole carriage turn into a hype squad. Pure magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you witness a crowd cheering as a tram breaks down in the middle of rush hour. It’s a mix of frustration and camaraderie that’s oddly heartwarming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by the way they know to avoid the Civic Center BART on Fridays. It’s not just the usual delays; it’s chaos and smoke every weekend.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Always amusing how the Muni driver at Civic Center interacts with panhandlers. One called for change, and the driver tossed a peanut butter sandwich out the window.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for the hipster vibe, but locals know the real treasure lies in the tiny, family-run bakeries hidden in the alleys.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by how they struggle to navigate the Mercat de Sant Antoni—clutching their guidebooks while locals breeze past.
